title: abutilon incanum
intro: Abutilon incanum, also known as the Indian mallow, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright green leaves and showy, pink or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Abutilon incanum include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural remedy for reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Abutilon incanum is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and can also be used topically as a paste or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
